ai_overview: The primary theme for yesterday (Sunday 17 May) was a total absence of activity across all functions, including your direct reports, the development squads, and the QA team. While this likely reflects a scheduled weekend or holiday lull, it means there has been no movement on the roadmap or resolution of existing tasks over the last 24 hours. Consequently, there are no new blockers or cross-team dependencies to flag, though the lack of data could also point to a synchronization issue with our tracking tools. You should confirm that this downtime was planned and ensure that the POs have a clear plan to resume velocity immediately today. It would be prudent to verify that no critical path items were delayed by this collective pause to keep our delivery timelines on track. Supplementary note: Jira returned zero GDCU ticket updates for 17 May and no tracked channel in Slack showed weekend posts. The last active day was Friday 15 May, when Chloe Christie shipped the DC9 Q2 Week 2 release, Harden Wallace and Novica Vukobratovic were coordinating a Cosmo Spins mobile hotfix in #gdc-pod, and Robin John and Shane McGrath completed release QA. Two items carry into Monday: the Cosmo Spins mobile reopening bug (game shrinks on repeated opens — flagged by Shane) and the auth modal orientation issue in landscape mode (flagged by Novica), both awaiting Harden's hotfix sign-off. Separately, an organisational 'at risk' communication was coordinated by Colin Brannigan and Michael Butlin on 12 May — division leads were asked to speak with their teams individually.

people.name: Vic Dadson
people.summary: No activity on 17 May (Sunday). No Slack or Jira activity visible from Vic across any tracked channel in recent weeks. Vic has been on doctor-signed medical sick leave since 21 April; no visible update on return date. Mike DM'd on 23 April with encouraging news about a data engineering conversation with Chris in the BI team — Nemanja and the data team will provide knowledge-sharing support on her return.

people.name: Jessica Dordevic
people.summary: No activity on 17 May (Sunday). Last visible Slack activity was 6 May in #gdc-product-design-pod, where she noted she was taking a look at a list of open design tickets Chloe flagged. On 7 May Ciara tagged her in #gdc-product-design-pod sharing the new Claude project assistant for HPC graphics (GDCU-8956) and updated documentation. Previously active on the Tinder Widget design (GDCU-8546); no Jira updates in last 24 hours.

people.name: Miguel Migneco
people.summary: No activity on 17 May (Sunday). Last visible Slack activity was 11 May in #gdc-product-led-growth — contributed to the Ace dark mode discussion, recommending against dark mode on a single page when the rest of the site does not have it ('if the rest of the site doesn't have dark mode then 1 page in the site shouldn't have darkmode'). Previously noted he was off on 6 May. No Jira updates in last 24 hours.

people.name: Ciara Carroll
people.summary: No activity on 17 May (Sunday). Most recent Slack activity was 15 May in #gdc-product-design-pod, responding briefly on a design query. On 12 May was unable to access the all-hands Zoom alongside Mike. On 8 May published a significant design system update in #gdc-product-led-growth — migrated sports team logos as Figma DS components, merged DS branches (Mini Brand Review, Registration Login, Slot Games with Game Provider Name), and published the updated style guide. On 7 May published the Claude project graphics assistant (GDCU-8956) for HPC banner creation in both #gdc-product-design-pod and #gdc-nonen-sync, with updated documentation covering Genesis media manager. No Jira updates in last 24 hours.

people.name: Josh Smith
people.summary: No activity on 17 May (Sunday). Last visible Slack activity was 1 May in #gdc-nonen-sync, announcing the new Card Carousel shortcode with Confluence documentation and a shortcodes test page link. Chloe Christie has been leading DC9 release communications. No Jira updates in last 24 hours. Earlier this month (7 May) Chloe asked Eoin to review GDCU-8839 in #6amigos; Josh is the likely owner of DC10 planning preparation.

people.name: Chloe Christie
people.summary: No activity on 17 May (Sunday). Most recent activity was Friday 15 May in #gdc-core, where she published the full DC9 Q2 Week 2 release announcement. New features shipped: In-App Age Gate (18+ compliance for FTP App), CA/FR Oplist Redesign with compare function and editors picks, ACE Navigation Redesign with global nav bar and improved sidebar, BI VIP RevShare A/B test, and Local Navigation for News Pages. WIP UX items noted: Sports Quiz Design System pressure test, Cosmo Spins World Cup Reskin, and World Cup Predictor Game. DC10 planned work previewed includes Tabs Component Image Field, Country Link Shortcode, AB Testing for UK Tenancy Removal, Standard.UK Adtech partnership, World Cup Cosmo Reskin, Viktor AI CWV recommendations for Online Casinos, and Rewards Banners. Also active 14 May in #gdc-product-design-pod (ticket planning for GDCU-8972) and #gdc-nonen-sync (NL 404 page query). No Jira updates in last 24 hours.

people.name: Carlos Blauth
people.summary: No activity on 17 May (Sunday). Last Slack activity was 14 May in #gdc-pod — investigating an issue where betting tips were unavailable for Irish (IE) users but working correctly from UK. Flagged it may be a cache issue and noted he would investigate. No Jira updates in last 24 hours.

people.name: Eoin McCarthy
people.summary: No activity on 17 May (Sunday). Last visible Slack activity was 7 May in #6amigos — reviewed GDCU-8839 for developer concerns and confirmed it looked good ('Looks good to me'). Named as a stakeholder on GDCU-8877 (phone number email confirmation form, now shipped in DC9 W1). No Jira updates in last 24 hours.

people.name: Harden Wallace
people.summary: No activity on 17 May (Sunday). Most recent Slack activity was 15 May in #gdc-pod — working on a Cosmo Spins mobile hotfix, confirmed the fix was working correctly on his own devices and requested iPhone testing from Shane McGrath and Robin John before releasing. Shane reported a persistent bug (game gets progressively smaller on each reopen), leaving the hotfix sign-off unresolved going into the weekend. Chloe also flagged in #gdc-product-design-pod on 15 May that Harden needs design support on GDCU-8958. No Jira updates in last 24 hours. Expect Harden to action both the hotfix decision and GDCU-8958 review on Monday.

people.name: Kelvin Yee
people.summary: No activity on 17 May (Sunday). Last visible channel activity was joining #gdc-f2p-apps on 15 April. No Slack or Jira activity visible in tracked channels since then. Last substantive DM context with Mike was 9 April — proposed using Site Article Categories with a 'Tips' tag to filter stale racing tips from the News Hub. No Jira updates in last 24 hours.

people.name: Milan Nikolic
people.summary: No activity on 17 May (Sunday). No Slack or Jira activity visible in tracked channels in recent weeks. Last DM context with Mike was 26 March (Global Strategy Hub — confirmed v1 will serve in English with localised casino results). No Jira updates in last 24 hours.

people.name: Nebojsa Stanic
people.summary: No activity on 17 May (Sunday). No Slack or Jira activity visible in any tracked channel in 2026. Last known involvement was a Tinder-style casino card prototype demo during the AI Day session in December 2025. No Jira updates in last 24 hours.

people.name: Novica Vukobratovic
people.summary: No activity on 17 May (Sunday). Most recent Slack activity was 15 May in #gdc-pod — reported his Cosmo Spins mobile fixes as complete and requested Harden's code review. Also logged two remaining open bugs: (1) after first game close, reopening leaves the game unclickable; (2) the auth modal appears in portrait orientation while the game is in landscape, requiring the user to double-rotate their phone to sign in and play. On 14 May posted a detailed CWV performance analysis: post-DC9 W2 release, UK online casinos page median mobile score improved by ~10–12 points (now ~60–74). No Jira updates in last 24 hours. Two open mobile bugs are expected to carry into Monday.

people.name: Sanja Budic
people.summary: No activity on 17 May (Sunday). No Slack or Jira activity visible in tracked channels since April. Last activity was 8–9 April in #gdc-deep-link-betslip-project — gaining access to Alex Kornilov's Figma design files for the embedded betslip / bet finder integration. Previously flagged a performance issue with the /api/mcp/deep_links endpoint for the Bookies brand (responses consistently above 5 seconds). No Jira updates in last 24 hours.

people.name: Robin John
people.summary: No activity on 17 May (Sunday). Most recent Slack activity was 14 May in #gdc-pod — confirmed QA sign-off for all tickets that went live that day ('QA have finished verifying the tickets which went live today and everything looks fine'). On 15 May Harden Wallace requested Robin test the Cosmo Spins hotfix on iPhone; no visible confirmation response in the captured data. No Jira updates in last 24 hours.

people.name: Shane McGrath
people.summary: No activity on 17 May (Sunday). Most recent Slack activity was 15 May in #gdc-pod — tested the Cosmo Spins hotfix on iPhone and reported a persistent bug where the game gets progressively smaller each time it is exited and reopened ('perfect on first try… it gets smaller and smaller'). On 14 May also flagged that the search icon no longer animates to the nav bar on scroll. Both issues remain open going into the weekend. No Jira updates in last 24 hours.

people.name: Dean Ryan
people.summary: No activity on 17 May (Sunday). Last visible Slack activity was 15 April in #gdc-f2p-apps, where he suggested rebranding the FTP mobile app as a 'Cosmo App' with a slots library and free spins offers. Gabriel Cornoiu relayed his feedback to the group. No visible channel or Jira activity since. Most recent DM context with Mike was 30 April (tipster success rate logging confirmed, odds formatting review for horse racing tips widgets). No Jira updates in last 24 hours.

people.name: Michael Butlin
people.summary: No activity on 17 May (Sunday). Most recent Slack activity was 12 May in #core-division-leads — actively engaged in the 'at risk' redundancy communication process coordinated by Colin Brannigan, clarifying the content of the at-risk email with division leads and directly with individuals. Notable quote: 'This is in the at risk email and what I am clarifying with people.' No Jira updates in last 24 hours.

people.name: Dan Smullen
people.summary: No activity on 17 May (Sunday). Last Slack activity was 15 April in #gdc-f2p-apps — flagged that some F2P games are not working and that Cosmo Spins showed a 24-hour wait on spins post-login. Gabriel Cornoiu noted the app and site share the same Cosmo Spins instance. No Jira or channel activity visible since. No Jira updates in last 24 hours.

people.name: Gabriel Cornoiu
people.summary: No activity on 17 May (Sunday). Most recent Slack activity was 5 May in #6amigos — gave Harden Wallace advance notice that Genesis would release the Brands Carousel component the following Thursday, just before the weekly release. On 1 May active in both #gdc-pdmt (available for morning DMs) and #gdc-nonen-sync (querying Josh about Card Carousel Confluence documentation). No Jira updates in last 24 hours.

people.name: Diana Thurmann
people.summary: No activity on 17 May (Sunday). Most recent Slack activity was 1 May in #gdc-nonen-sync — clarified that Josh's Card Carousel shortcode announcement includes a link to the Confluence docs. Prior to that, the Iceland geo launch announcement on 16 April in #core-division (gambling.com/is and /is/en live). Last DM context with Mike was 22 April (Clarity API key and 10 calls/day usage limit for Viktor-based analytics automation). No Jira updates in last 24 hours.

people.name: Colin Brannigan
people.summary: No activity on 17 May (Sunday). Most recent Slack activity was 14 May in #core-division-leads — identified IE/SE/CA/IN as key starting geos for prediction markets rollout, with Commercial keen to start planning. Earlier that week (12–13 May) coordinated a significant organisational change communication: issued 'at risk' emails to the division, created a HR FAQ resource in Cowork, and advised division leads to have individual conversations with their teams. Confirmed contractors would not receive the notification. Context to be aware of: some team members may still be processing this communication as they return Monday. No Jira updates in last 24 hours.
